texto: I know now that the poem in my head, the one that pushed me to the page, begged me - or dared me to be born is almost never the poem that comes out. I suppose it’s like anything born of/with free will and the will to live: once I’ve given the seed, once the juices flow through any sort of birth canal and make it to the ambient air there will, at that point, be forces that come into play that are no longer entirely mine. To forget that each word is a life unto itself is to strangle it dead before it can even take a step.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Juan-Paolo Perre: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I know now that the poem in my head, the one that pushed me to the page, begged me - or dared me …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, poem, poetry pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de poetry e the-life-of-words.
